issue.title: www.bestbuy.com

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.bestbuy.com/site/surge-protectors-power-cords/battery-backup/abcat0515043.c?id=abcat0515043](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bestbuy.com%2Fsite%2Fsurge-protectors-power-cords%2Fbattery-backup%2Fabcat0515043.c%3Fid%3Dabcat0515043) ### Comment > Problem Description > When browsing product listing pages (specifically the Battery Backup/UPS category), the webpage experiences severe layout shifting, massive rendering lag, and leaves large unrendered white placeholder squares where promotional or sponsored elements are supposed to live. > > The issue causes a significant waiting period/timeout delay, which temporarily freezes browser interaction. > > Environment > Browser: Google Chrome (Latest Version) > > Extensions: AdGuard Browser Assistant > > Desktop Client: AdGuard for Windows/Mac (System-wide interception enabled) > > Active Filter: AdGuard Base Filter > > Steps to Reproduce > Enable the AdGuard Base Filter in the AdGuard desktop application. > > Open Google Chrome (reproducible in both standard and Incognito sessions). > > Navigate to any heavy retail listing page, such as the Best Buy UPS Battery Backup Section. > > Observe the significant delay in page load speed and the prominent empty white blocks scattered across the layout. > > Expected Behavior > AdGuard should perform network-level blocking alongside cosmetic filtering to cleanly collapse the elements. The page layout should snap together seamlessly without placeholder blocks or script-induced connection timeouts. > > Actual Behavior / Workaround > Disabling the AdGuard Base Filter or adding a custom user exclusion rule `(@@||bestbuy.com^$document)` completely resolves the performance tanking and restores normal site rendering, isolating the issue directly to a structural rule within the base filter list. ``` Username: @CosmicStarCoder ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/cs14dt7h1pcgoscwc0w4oo8ccw4ks4spizh5mcgddc.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/1njik3rrjosgwc008040sg0ww80gg8cenqivmkgvhs.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Windows v7.22.7 (5271) System version: | `Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26200.0` Browser: | Chrome License type: | paid AdGuard driver: | WFP Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter,
AdGuard Mobile Ads filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter,
AdGuard Other Annoyances filter,
AdGuard Widgets filter

**Security:**
Online Malicious URL Blocklist Tracking protection: | **General**:
Block trackers,
Hide your search queries,
Send Do-Not-Track header

**Tracking Methods**:

### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://www.imdb.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.imdb.com%2F) ### Comment > No one asked for a red notification-style badge ticking up whenever a new thing is blocked. Not one person in the world wants their adblocker to be eye-catching. The fact that it can't be disabled is very disappointing. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/psnxz3mbqr4owg8k0g0s0oggos8sg4s9b8m7qkkjmk.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Mini for Mac v2.2.0.1052 System version: | `MacOS 26.5 (Build 25F71)` Browser: | Safari License type: | free Advanced rules: | enabled Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Social Widgets:**
AdGuard Social Media filter,
Fanboy's Social Blocking List

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter,
AdGuard Other Annoyances filter,
AdGuard Widgets filter

**Security:**
Online Malicious URL Blocklist Filters last update: | 2026-06-01T00:05:22 Userscripts: | disabled Userstyles: | disabled

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://www.paramountplus.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.paramountplus.com%2F)

### Comment
> I am using 94.140.14.14 and 94.140.15.15 for DNS on my router and dns.adguard-dns.com on my android phone.  Recently we have starting experiencing issues with the android and streaming device apps for Paramount Plus, though they worked without issue in the past.  I don't use this app regularly, though fairly sure I had used it about 4-6 weeks ago without any issues. I do have an ad free account with Paramount Plus, though they often play an advertisement for their content before a program starts. 
> 
> There's no issue browsing to Paramount Plus via the web and watching videos.  I am also able to navigate the site without issue within the Paramount plus app on the phone or streaming device until I attempt to play a video which then sits at a blank window until eventually failing. If I do not use the AdGuard DNS filter playing video in the Paramount Plus apps works without issue.  If you need any additional information from me, or have anything you would like me to try, feel free to let me know.
> 

```
Username: @TheCarsForever
```


### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/o74hx9jun9cwg4ok4kggw8ogk4s4wg4zes5wd317wo.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/sx0wtkx3sdckokcwokwgkocgw40os4olc2ccz18n5s.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default
Browser: | Chrome

comment.body: @TheCarsForever DNS filtering is not flexible. Thats why we suggested a private DNS, where you can unblock domains only for a specific device.

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

New change of tinyShield's build script for Apple Safari Extension user (made at 2026 Apr 21) is available.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://sss.vn.ua/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Fsss.vn.ua%2F) ### Comment > Adguard thinks that site http://sss.vn.ua is phishing site, but it is just another old address of my site https://scriptures.ru.
